title: MD
biography: Baruj Benacerraf was a Venezuelan-American immunologist who shared the Nobel Prize in 1980 for discovering the major histocompatibility complex (MHC) genes that regulate immune responses. His work on immune response genes was foundational to understanding organ transplant rejection and autoimmune diseases.
aiSummary: Baruj Benacerraf was an immunology specialist with an H-index of 70 at Harvard Medical School. Nobel Prize 1980 for discovering MHC genes regulating immune responses. Based in Boston, United States.

citations.title: Role of the major histocompatibility complex in the immune response

awards: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (1980)

timeline.title: Discovered immune response (Ir) genes
timeline.description: Identified genetic factors controlling the ability to mount immune responses to specific antigens

timeline.title: Linked Ir genes to MHC
timeline.description: Demonstrated that immune response genes are part of the major histocompatibility complex

timeline.title: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine
timeline.description: Shared with Jean Dausset and George Snell for discoveries concerning MHC genes
